core_loop: Province (17) contains Settlements (37, S-001..S-037). Governor gets one mandatory seasonal verb (Develop/Fortify/Pacify/Administer, §3.2) targeting Prosperity/Defense/Order; Order→province Accord (floor-mean, §1.3). Settlement L/PS (§1.8 LPS-2e) aggregate size-weighted into faction Mandate, which mean-reverts L/PS back (damped, sim-bounded 0-7/30 seasons). Local Actors + settlement events feed the Scene Slate. Subnational factions can be granted management (§3.3); Thread ops nudge settlement stats automatically (§4.4); Thread Exploitation Sites are a discoverable harvest tap (§4.9).

decision_points.note: single stat-pump, no political cost or fork; self-diagnosed by governance_play_redesign_v1 as 'roll one die a season'; no GM exists to compensate (CLAUDE.md header)

decision_points.options: grant to Church/Guild/Ministry/Löwenritter/RM/Wardens vs retain direct control
decision_points.note: real tradeoff, but rare Domain Action, flat Ob not costed against alternatives
three well-costed tradeoffs; fires only on a narrow trigger

decision_points.options: wait for departure / expand capacity / accept Prince-in-Waiting + recurring contest
harvest or don't (no contest, no cap)
decision_points.note: uncapped, uncontested Wealth+1/RS-0.5 faucet once discovered

decision_points.options: AP allocation across 8 method-forcing verbs; Directive comply/bargain/defy; Petition/Friction/Crisis event-deck responses
decision_points.note: not the ratified loop — absent from CURRENT.md, gated on unbuilt settlement registry (§5.2 G1)
shared with mass-battle lane, not settlement-governance-specific

north_star.collision_feed: Local Actors (~45-50, Conviction+Disposition) and settlement events feed Scene Slate P4/P5; the Mandate<->L/PS loop is a genuine cross-scale (Ω-a) channel. But most collision fodder comes from passive state, not named player tradeoffs — that gap is exactly what the unratified redesign targets.
north_star.verdict: The ratified settlement-governance loop does not yet point at the North Star (thin/degenerate seasonal cadence); a well-aimed fix is drafted but unratified, untracked, and gated on unbuilt sim infrastructure, while the Mandate math and adjacency/siege machinery around it are comparatively solid.

nq.observation: Size-weighted settlement L/PS -> saturating faction Mandate models a real Renaissance dynamic (legitimacy scales with population/wealth actually held); CK3/EU4/Victoria/KOEI anchors cited in-doc.
Mandate formula 7T/(T+K) is explicitly saturating/anti-runaway and Stage-4-sim-verified bounded 0-7 over 30 seasons under mission shocks — solid non-dominance evidence.

Dramatic-legibility fails for the seasonal governance action (one verb, one roll, no named-actor stakes) but passes for the RM Governance Transition scene and the unratified Directive comply/bargain/defy fork.

settlement_adjacency_v30.md's own worked hub/edge examples cite pre-PP-726 settlement IDs (S-015 Gransol, S-023 Himmelenger, S-026 Sigurdshelm) that contradict the actual canonical YAML, which already uses post-renumbering IDs (S-018/S-036/S-031, verified directly).

threadwork_junctures.juncture: Relational+ Thread ops -> settlement stat deltas (Weaving/Pulling/Mending/etc, ±1/season cap)
Thread Exploitation Sites (Proximity ≤2) harvestable by any actor
Settlement-governance verb to regulate/exploit/seal a Thread site
threadwork_junctures.evidence: §3.2 verb menu has no Thread-response verb despite §4.4/§4.9 existing as automatic triggers — the practitioner acts, the governor never gets to; sharper scope than the already-tracked temperament gap

threadwork_junctures.juncture: Governance-deck 'Thread' card family (RS bleed, Calamity edge)
threadwork_junctures.evidence: governance_play_redesign_v1.md §2.3 — but PROPOSAL-only, gated on G1, not in CURRENT.md
territory_temperaments_v30.md — Calamity-adjacency flavor correlation only (T6/T13/T15 seeded near outcomes-only), zero live coupling; KNOWN-TRACKED per 03_threadwork_surface.md §3

legibility.note: Whose-position-at-risk: answerable at the faction/Mandate tier (§1.8) but not at the settlement-governor tier in ratified state (no suspicion/recall clock — that's governance_play_redesign §1.4, unratified). Actor-wants: Local Actors have one Conviction but no ambition/goal (unratified NPC ambition schema would fix this, §3.1 of the proposal). What-happens-if-no-one-acts: answerable cleanly (§4.3 stat-threshold events: Prosperity 0->famine, Order 0->revolt).

degenerate_candidates: settlement_layer_v30.md §3.2 — single mandatory governance verb-pick, no political cost, self-diagnosed in governance_play_redesign_v1.md's problem statement
settlement_layer_v30.md §4.9 — Thread Exploitation Site harvesting: uncapped, uncontested, no depletion, a free recurring Wealth+1/RS-0.5 tap

edges.mechanism: Fort Level->Defender Ob, siege/assault/bypass, Manoeuvre modifiers
none found — zero mechanical consumption of α/β in settlement_layer or module_contracts
edges.cite: territory_temperaments_v30.md vs settlement_layer_v30.md (no cross-reference either way)

edges.mechanism: AP verbs, Directive, event deck, NPC ambition would replace §3.2

directions.top-down: Ω/N pass cleanly at the Mandate-math layer, but the seasonal verb-pick contradicts the corpus's own stated design philosophy that 'governing is choosing under constraint.'
directions.bottom-up: §1.8's formulas are Stage-4 sim-verified bounded over 30 seasons; the §3.2 verb menu has no comparable sim backing and its calibration items (ED-SETT-01/02) remain DEFERRED.
directions.lateral: territory_temperaments (province-grain, uniform-weighted, PROVISIONAL) sits orthogonal to settlement-grain governance with no mechanical bridge in either direction.
directions.diagonal: settlement<->faction Mandate is the strongest cross-scale channel in this lane; settlement<->thread is present but mechanically inert as a governor-facing choice.
directions.forwards: governance_play_redesign_v1 is a correctly-aimed forward path but is gated on an unbuilt settlement registry (G1) and is absent from both CURRENT.md and HANDOFF_SE.md's (empty) pending list — a real risk of falling through the cracks.
directions.backwards: settlement_adjacency_v30.md's worked examples still cite pre-PP-726 settlement IDs that no longer match the canonical YAML or settlement_layer's renumbered registry, despite both being authored the same day.

findings.claim: The ratified seasonal governance loop is a single stat-pump verb-pick with no political tradeoff, self-diagnosed by the corpus's own redesign proposal as collapsing to 'roll one die a season' — and Valoria has no GM to compensate.

governance_play_redesign_v1.md is a well-aimed fix for the P1 loop above but is absent from CURRENT.md's settlement/territory head list and from HANDOFF_SE.md's pending items (which log 'none'), risking permanent orphan status alongside its unbuilt G1 prerequisite.

Thread Exploitation Sites are an uncapped, uncontested income tap (Wealth+1/RS-0.5 per season) once discovered, with no depletion or rival-actor contest specified — a degenerate line and a missed collision-engine opportunity.

Settlement governance has no verb to respond to Thread ops/exploitation at its own location — Thread affects the settlement automatically but the governor never gets a choice about it; sharper scope than the already-tracked temperament-thread absence.
findings.tracked_ref: 03_threadwork_surface.md §3 (territory_temperaments absence) — this finding extends that to settlement §3.2's verb menu itself

findings.claim: settlement_adjacency_v30.md's own worked hub/edge examples cite pre-PP-726 settlement IDs that contradict the actual canonical adjacency data, which already uses the post-renumbering IDs.

Two of the three dramatic-legibility questions (whose position is at risk; what each actor wants) cannot be answered from ratified settlement-tier state alone — only the third (what happens if no one acts) can.
findings.cite: designs/territory/settlement_layer_v30.md §3.2/§4.3/§4.5 (Local Actors, one Conviction, no ambition field)

findings.claim: Territory temperament α/β has zero mechanical consumption anywhere in the ratified settlement layer or module_contracts, not just with respect to Thread — broader inertness than the already-tracked thread-specific gap.
findings.cite: designs/territory/territory_temperaments_v30.md vs designs/territory/settlement_layer_v30.md and references/module_contracts.yaml (no temperament references found)
findings.tracked_ref: 03_threadwork_surface.md §3 (thread-specific); this finding broadens to full mechanical inertness
